projectDescription: Sample project demonstrating how to build multitenant applications with Java 21 and Spring Boot 3.2, featuring schema-per-tenant databases, per-tenant OAuth2/OIDC authentication, and tenant-isolated vector store queries.
keyFeatures: Schema-per-tenant PostgreSQL isolation using Hibernate MultiTenantConnectionProvider
Per-tenant OAuth2/OIDC authentication via dynamic Keycloak realm selection
Subdomain-based tenant routing with Spring Cloud Gateway
Tenant-isolated vector store queries in ChromaDB using metadata filtering
Tenant-aware cache key generation to prevent cross-tenant data leakage
Tenant ID propagated to MDC (logs) and Micrometer traces for full observability
RAG (Retrieval-Augmented Generation) with per-tenant document isolation using Spring AI
publicApiSurface: GET /instruments — List all instruments for the current tenant
GET /instruments/{type} — List instruments by type (cached per tenant)
POST /instruments — Create a new instrument in the current tenant's schema
POST /ai/simple/chat — Send a plain message to the LLM (Ollama/llama2)
POST /ai/doc/chat — Send a message with RAG using tenant-filtered ChromaDB documents
TenantContextHolder — Thread-local store for current tenant identifier
TenantDetailsService — Interface for loading tenant configuration
TenantContextFilter — OncePerRequestFilter that resolves and sets tenant context
TenantAuthenticationManagerResolver — Per-tenant JWT validation against correct Keycloak realm
TenantClientRegistrationRepository — Reactive per-tenant OAuth2 OIDC client registration
TenantFlywayMigrationInitializer — Runs Flyway migrations per tenant schema
TenantKeyGenerator — Cache key generator prefixed with tenant ID
